template.name: Technical Review
template.description: Structured checklist for code reviews, pre-launch reviews, architecture reviews, accessibility audits, security evaluations, and similar technical evaluations.

tasks.name: Jira ticket exists, is assigned, and reflects current status
Type of review is explicitly identified
tasks.notes: e.g., code review, pre-launch, architecture, accessibility, security

tasks.name: Artifact or deliverable under review is identified and linked or accessible
Reviewer(s) are assigned and aware of the assignment
tasks.notes: Assigned specifically — not just "available".

tasks.name: Review is authorized
tasks.notes: The work is at a stage where review is appropriate and expected.

tasks.name: Review scope is bounded
tasks.notes: State up front what is and is not being evaluated.

tasks.name: Acceptance criteria are defined
tasks.notes: What does a passing review look like?

tasks.name: Review is scoped against documented requirements
tasks.notes: Not against personal preference or convention alone.

tasks.name: Access to required environments, branches, staging, or artifacts is confirmed
tasks.notes: Confirm access before starting — not mid-review.

tasks.name: Dependencies are noted
tasks.notes: e.g., specific browsers, device types, data sets, third-party integrations

tasks.name: At least one known risk or concern is identified before review begins
tasks.notes: What are you specifically looking for?

tasks.name: Time window for the review is established and communicated
Review is conducted against the stated scope and acceptance criteria
Findings are documented as they are identified
tasks.notes: Not reconstructed from memory afterward.

tasks.name: Each finding is tagged with a severity or priority level
tasks.notes: blocking, major, minor, or suggestion

tasks.name: Findings distinguish between required changes and recommendations
Questions are surfaced to the original author
tasks.notes: Do not assume or guess — ask.

tasks.name: No out-of-scope changes are introduced during the review
Accessibility, performance, or security concerns are flagged explicitly if relevant
tasks.notes: Flag explicitly — do not let these get absorbed into general findings without a label.

tasks.name: Cross-browser or cross-device behavior is checked where applicable
Open findings are tracked to resolution, not just logged
tasks.notes: Runs alongside Executing — not a one-time gate.

tasks.name: Fixes are verified against the original finding
tasks.notes: Confirm the issue is resolved, not just changed.

tasks.name: Significant revisions trigger a re-review of the affected area
Scope drift is watched for
tasks.notes: New issues introduced during revision go back through the finding process.

tasks.name: Review timeline is monitored
tasks.notes: If it's slipping, surface it — don't absorb silently.

tasks.name: All findings are resolved or formally accepted with documented rationale
Approval is documented in Jira
tasks.notes: Not just communicated verbally.

tasks.name: Items deferred out of scope have follow-on tickets created
tasks.notes: Create tickets with clear descriptions — do not lose track of deferred items.

tasks.name: Status updated to Done in Jira
tasks.notes: Or appropriate terminal state.

tasks.name: One-sentence lessons learned or retrospective note logged in the ticket
